answer:
The individual has logarithmic utility, so utility is \(U(W) = \ln(W)\). With full insurance at an actuarially fair rate, the premium equals the expected loss: \(0.5 \times 10,000 = 5,000\). After paying the premium, wealth is \(20,000 - 5,000 = 15,000\), and utility is \(\ln(15,000) \approx 9.616\).  

\boxed{B}
